notes_md:
**ngược lại** = *on the contrary* / *the opposite is true*. It shows a contrast where the second idea goes against the first one.

It’s a linking phrase between two clauses, often in the middle of a sentence. You use it to flip the expectation.


Close meaning: **trái lại** (very similar, slightly more formal). Think of it as “reverse direction” in logic — you turn the idea around.

translation.en: I think the opposite is true.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He likes coffee, on the contrary I only drink tea every morning.
She thinks this math problem is hard, on the contrary I find it quite easy.
Everyone thought it would be sunny, on the contrary it rained heavily today.

example.en: I think that is not true; on the contrary, I believe it can happen.
example.es: Creo que eso no es cierto; al contrario, creo que puede suceder.
example.vi: Tôi nghĩ rằng điều đó không đúng, ngược lại, tôi tin rằng nó có thể xảy ra.

definitions.en: In opposition to what has been stated; on the contrary.
definitions.es: En oposición a lo que se ha afirmado; al contrario.
definitions.vi: Ngược lại với những gì đã được nói; trái lại.

content.sentence: Ý kiến của họ ___ với tôi.

translation.en: Their opinion is the opposite of mine.

exampleSentences: Anh ấy thích cà phê, ngược lại em chỉ uống trà mỗi sáng.
Chị nghĩ bài toán này khó, ngược lại em thấy nó khá dễ.
Mọi người nghĩ trời sẽ nắng, ngược lại hôm nay lại mưa rất to.
notes:
ngược lại = on the contrary / the opposite is true. It shows a contrast where the second idea goes against the first one.

It’s a linking phrase between two clauses, often in the middle of a sentence. You use it to flip the expectation.

Close meaning: trái lại (very similar, slightly more formal). Think of it as “reverse direction” in logic — you turn the idea around.
